prerequisites: A GNews account. Sign up for free at gnews.io and verify your email address.
A GNews API key obtained from your GNews dashboard after registration.
A search query string for the `query` (q) parameter, which is required for the search endpoint (max 200 characters).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: GNews authenticates every request with an API key. The connector's `api_key` secret field holds this key. GNews accepts it either as the `apikey` query parameter (https://gnews.io/api/v4/{endpoint}?apikey=YOUR_API_KEY) or via the `X-Api-Key` HTTP header. A missing or invalid key returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized.

setupSteps.title: Create a GNews account and get your API key
setupSteps.body: Sign up for free at gnews.io, confirm the verification email, then open your dashboard to retrieve your API key. Paste this value into the connector's `api_key` field.

setupSteps.title: Set the required search query
setupSteps.body: Provide the `query` field, which maps to the GNews search endpoint's required `q` keyword parameter (maximum 200 characters).

setupSteps.title: Narrow the search with optional filters
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `language` (2-letter language code), `country` (2-letter country code), and `in` to choose which article attributes are searched. Accepted `in` values are `title`, `description`, and `content`, and they can be combined with commas (for example `title,description`).

setupSteps.title: Set the date range and sort order
setupSteps.body: Optionally restrict results with `start_date` and `end_date`, which map to the GNews `from` and `to` parameters and must be in ISO 8601 format (for example 2025-07-18T21:32:58.500Z). Set `sortby` to either `publishedAt` (newest first) or `relevance` (best keyword match). Use `nullable` to declare which attributes may be returned as null.

setupSteps.title: Configure top headlines (optional)
setupSteps.body: To pull trending articles from the top-headlines endpoint, set `top_headlines_topic`, which maps to the GNews `category` parameter. Accepted values are general, world, nation, business, technology, entertainment, sports, science, and health (default general). Use `top_headlines_query` to add keyword filtering on top headlines.
